response.summary: Fail. The implemented flow appears to cover the core structure for opening items and showing detail content, but the user-facing semantics do not satisfy the contract because visible copy exposes internal product/process language. The clearest blocker is onboarding text that says 'in this slice' and lists excluded features, which is not acceptable user-facing copy. There is also supporting copy that explains implementation mechanics rather than user value, and the item-load error state is too generic to help users understand which item failed.

findings.observed: Visible copy says: 'Only age and gender are collected in this slice. No family mode, reminders, or item editing are included.' This directly exposes internal slice/scope language and implementation boundaries instead of user-facing guidance.

Generating screen helper says: 'The plan is created locally before the dashboard appears.' This explains system mechanics rather than user value, which conflicts with the contract guidance for explanatory copy.

Welcome/onboarding lead copy includes phrases like 'self-only flow' and 'rule-based dashboard,' which sound product-internal and process-oriented rather than helping the user understand their benefit in plain language.

When a detail item cannot be loaded, the screen uses the generic heading 'Health item details' and does not show the item title fallback if available. This weakens recovery because users cannot easily tell which health item failed to open.
